Acceptance Criteria: Block expressions plus if/match in value position parse, type-check, lower through the existing verified path, and ship with syntax/spec/compatibility tests without touching prom-*.

match guards and guard clauses lower to explicit control flow with deterministic evaluation order, unsupported targets produce precise diagnostics, and verified-path tests cover success and failure cases.
Pipeline operator and expression-bodied functions desugar to existing call and return semantics, keep left-to-right determinism, and expose desugaring notes in spec/tests.
Capture-free short lambdas plus wildcard/discard patterns ship with explicit v0 limits, no alternate runtime path, and parser/sema/tests/docs for call-site use.
Range literals, for-over-range, and compound assignment lower through canonical loop and read-modify-write IR, preserve current quota/type behavior, and have verified-path coverage.
Named arguments reorder cleanly onto existing positional call semantics, diagnostics cover unknown and duplicate names, and public docs define mixed-call legality.
assert(expr) lowers to the existing fail-fast core path, desugaring visibility is available in tooling/docs, and verified execution tests prove deterministic failure reporting.
const declarations and expanded numeric literal forms parse and type-check honestly, compile-time restrictions are explicit, and canonical literal/const docs are updated.
Tuple literals, tuple types, and tuple return values work end-to-end through SemCode and VM with a narrow canonical representation and verified-path coverage.
Tuple destructuring binds, destructuring assignment, and let-else lower through explicit temporaries and guard-style exits, preserve deterministic order, and report arity/target errors clearly.

Issue: Record value model and immutable update
Acceptance Criteria: Canonical record values, field access, and copy-with update syntax ship with one runtime representation, deterministic update semantics, and verified-path plus VM tests.
Algebraic sum types parse, type-check, lower, and execute through one canonical tagged representation with docs and tests for constructors and case analysis.
match over canonical ADT, Result, and Option surfaces enforces exhaustiveness with precise diagnostics and no silent fallback behavior.
Result and Option become first-class standard forms with canonical constructors, match ergonomics, and verified execution tests over success/none/error flows.
Core numeric types can carry a first-wave unit annotation, unit mismatches are compile-time errors, and docs define conversion/non-conversion rules honestly.
First-wave requires/ensures/invariant surface lowers to explicit contract checks or compile-time restrictions without hidden runtime semantics, and diagnostics/spec/tests are canonical.
Default parameters bind at call sites with deterministic evaluation order, remain compatible with named arguments, and docs/tests define explicit versus defaulted behavior.
Canonical schema declarations for records, tagged unions, and config/API models exist, stay separate from prom-* integration, and are gated cleanly through profile/edition rules.

Issue: Validation derived from schemas and types
Acceptance Criteria: Validation logic can be derived from canonical schema/type declarations, generated checks are deterministic and inspectable, and error docs/specs cover generated validation failures.
Typed config schemas support parse plus validation for canonical config files, diagnostics are user-facing and stable, and no alternate config truth layer is introduced.
Canonical schemas can generate stable API contract artifacts without hand-maintained duplicates, and generated outputs are clearly versioned and reproducible.
Schema versions, compatibility rules, and migration metadata are explicit, diffable, and testable, with stable docs for additive versus breaking evolution.

Issue: Tagged wire unions and patch types
Acceptance Criteria: Tagged wire-format unions and partial/patch types derive from canonical declarations, preserve determinism, and keep serialization/update semantics explicit in docs and tests.
